title: EHR security comparative-study dataset
description: Frozen schema for Paper #1. Paper #2's healthcare-adjusted index is a function over these tables. Every record carries snapshot_date and a provenance pointer to its data/raw source.

conventions.provenance: relative path under data/raw/ to the verbatim source response
conventions.nulls: use null for unknown/undeterminable values; never impute

practice_scores.description: Construct A. One row per (system, repo, Scorecard check).

fields.assessable: boolean (false for GitHub-only checks on Codeberg-hosted GNU Health)

disclosed_vuln.description: Construct B (landscape). One row per disclosed vulnerability matched to a system. `source` records the disclosure channel: most rows are CVE-ecosystem (nvd), but some systems disclose outside CVE (vendor_SA, release_notes) -- central to the RQ2 disclosure-asymmetry argument.

fields.vuln_id: string (CVE-..., GHSA-..., or a vendor advisory id)
fields.source: enum: nvd | osv | ghsa | vendor_SA | release_notes | bug_tracker
fields.severity: enum: low | medium | high | critical | null
fields.summary: string | null (one-line description; populated for curated/vendor rows)
fields.remediation_days: integer | null (OPPORTUNISTIC; null if fix not linkable)

dependency.description: Construct C. One row per (system, dependency, version).

fields.max_severity: enum: low | medium | high | critical | none

shared_dependency.description: Construct C centerpiece. One row per dependency shared by >1 system.

fields.max_severity: enum: low | medium | high | critical | none

descriptor.description: Construct D (CONTEXT, not an RQ). One row per system. No inferential use at N=5.

fields.security_policy_source: enum: repo_file | external_docs | null (where the coordinated-disclosure policy lives)
fields.security_policy_ref: string | null (repo path or external URL of the policy)
